pkgmetadata.longdescription: ListCrunchA simple human-readable way to compress redundant sequential data. Examplepythonfrom listcrunch import crunchcompressed_string crunch([1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 2]) Returns 10-829, meaning 1 appears in indices 0-8 (inclusive), and 2 occurs at index 9.To uncompress, use the uncrunch function.pythonfrom listcrunch import uncrunchuncrunch(500-1,3-432,5606707-8) Returns [50, 50, 3, 50, 50, 3, 60, 70, 70]
